disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A firm, hard, rough-surfaced lump on the sole of the foot present for several months, originally assumed to be a callus, with a few tiny dark specks visible within it
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe the lump — how long has it been there and what does it look like?
Has the lump itself changed, or just the pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a hard, rough little lump on the ball of my foot that's been there for months — I thought it was just a callus. If I look closely there are a few tiny dark specks in it. It hasn't really changed; it's the pain that came on today.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain is present only on direct vertical weight-bearing pressure and is completely absent at rest
trigger_question_examples: When exactly does it hurt — all the time, or only when you stand on it?
Does it hurt when you rest, or only when you put weight on it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It only hurts when I press straight down on it or stand on it — like stepping on glass. When I'm off my feet there's no pain at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Warning features of a deep or spreading infection — spreading redness, heat, swelling, pus or weeping, a continuous throbbing pain even at rest, or fever/chills — are being screened for and are currently absent
trigger_question_examples: Is the skin around the lump becoming hot, red, swollen, or leaking any pus?
Do you have any fever or chills, or a throbbing ache that carries on even when you rest?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — there's no spreading redness, heat, swelling or pus, and no fever or chills. It doesn't throb when I rest; it's only painful when I actually stand on it.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I'm mostly frightened it's something serious buried in my foot or an infection — should I be going to hospital tonight?

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No diabetes or nerve problems in the feet, normal feeling in the foot, and otherwise systemically well
trigger_question_examples: Do you have diabetes, poor circulation, or any numbness or reduced feeling in your feet?
Can you feel the lump and the skin around it normally?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No diabetes and no circulation or nerve problems that I know of — I can feel everything normally, and if anything this thing is painfully sensitive, not numb.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Marked functional impact — limping and walking on the outer edge of the foot to avoid pressure on the lesion
trigger_question_examples: How is this affecting your walking?
Are you able to weight-bear normally?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm hobbling — I have to walk on the side of my foot because putting weight on the lump is unbearable.
